methodology.size: Sum of first-load app shell files. Current JS includes public/index.html, CSS, JS modules, manifest and service worker. Dioxus includes generated benchmarks/dioxus/dist/public files excluding precompressed .br/.gz duplicates.
methodology.performance: Playwright Chromium median of 5 measured runs after 2 warmups. readyMs waits for the exercise picker chips to exist in the DOM.
methodology.fixture: Current JS is served with /api/data stubbed from benchmarks/data/sample-data.json. Dioxus uses equivalent static in-code data.

interpretation.size: The Dioxus/WASM benchmark is about 5.9x larger raw, 7.4x larger gzip and 6.8x larger brotli than the current JS frontend shell.
interpretation.performance: On this small fixture, Dioxus reaches the benchmark-ready UI slightly faster, but it also renders a simpler DOM and ships much more code. Treat this as a local overhead comparison, not proof that a full rewrite would be faster.
